steps.title: Find the intercept value
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs when $x=0$. Evaluate the original function at that input.
$$f(0)=5^{-4(0+1)}=5^{-4}=\frac{1}{625}$$
So the y-coordinate of the y-intercept is $\frac{1}{625}$.

steps.title: Rewrite the function
steps.content:
Expand and separate the exponent.
$$5^{-4(x+1)}=5^{-4x-4}=5^{-4}\cdot5^{-4x}$$
Since $5^{-4}=\frac{1}{625}$ and $5^{-4x}=(\frac{1}{5})^{4x}$, the function can be written as
$$f(x)=\frac{1}{625}(\frac{1}{5})^{4x}$$

steps.title: Identify the displayed form
steps.content: This form displays the y-intercept value, $\frac{1}{625}$, as the coefficient. The answer is A.
